name: Hub tab perf
description: RUM dashboard for Initiative 0017 (Hub Settings & Reports mount perf). Aggregates HUB_TAB_SWITCH_PERF events emitted by apps/web/src/core/lib/hubPerf.ts: P50/P95 ttiMs per tab, long-task burden, cache-hit ratio, histogram of ttiMs, and longTaskCount P95 per tab. Runbook: docs/observability/hub-perf-baseline.md. Baseline pull target: 2026-05-27 (≥ 7 days after Sprint 0 merge 2026-05-20).

payload.ttiMs: number — ms from hubView commit → TabReadyProbe mounted past 2× rAF
payload.longTaskMs: number — sum of `longtask` PerformanceEntry durations observed during the window
payload.cacheHit: boolean — chunk already in module cache when switch began

hub_tab_switch_perf.sampling: 100% for first 30 days post-merge, then 10% — gated via SAMPLE_RATE in hubPerf.ts + feature flag `hub_perf_rum_sample_rate`
hub_tab_switch_perf.notes: Dashboard `tab` does NOT include `dashboard` — eager-mount, no Suspense boundary, no measurement.

panels.name: Tab-switch TTI — P50 / P95 per tab
panels.description: P50 + P95 of `ttiMs` over the last 14 days, broken down by `tab`. P95 is the load-bearing number — Initiative 0017 acceptance gates Sprint 1/2/3 targets on P95 thresholds for Settings (≤7000/3000/1500 ms) and Reports (no explicit P95 in spec; tracked alongside).
panels.rationale: P50 alone hides the tail. Sprint 0 audit data (live audit 2026-05-20) measured one user one moment at ~10s for Settings — but the real distribution might be bimodal (cache-hit miss = catastrophic, cache-hit hit = fast). P95 is what users tweet about.

query.query: SELECT properties.tab AS tab, quantile(0.50)(toFloat(properties.ttiMs)) AS p50_ttiMs, quantile(0.95)(toFloat(properties.ttiMs)) AS p95_ttiMs, count() AS sample_size FROM events WHERE event = 'hub_tab_switch_perf' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 14 DAY GROUP BY tab ORDER BY tab

breakdown.comment: Web vs ios vs android. Mobile JS-execution is ~3× slower per Initiative 0017 §Чому зараз — expect mobile-platform P95 to be the worst quartile.

panels.name: Long-task burden — avg longTaskMs per tab
panels.description: Average sum-of-longtasks per tab-switch over the last 14 days. Long-tasks (≥50 ms) block the main thread; aggregate per switch tells us how much of the perceived freeze is actual main-thread blocking vs network/render.
panels.rationale: ttiMs measures user-visible delay; longTaskMs measures the cause. If ttiMs drops Sprint-over-Sprint but longTaskMs stays high → we shifted the freeze to a different point in the render tree, not eliminated it.

query.query: SELECT properties.tab AS tab, avg(toFloat(properties.longTaskMs)) AS avg_longTaskMs, quantile(0.95)(toFloat(properties.longTaskMs)) AS p95_longTaskMs, avg(toFloat(properties.longTaskCount)) AS avg_longTaskCount, quantile(0.95)(toFloat(properties.longTaskCount)) AS p95_longTaskCount FROM events WHERE event = 'hub_tab_switch_perf' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 14 DAY GROUP BY tab ORDER BY tab

panels.name: Cache-hit ratio per tab
panels.description: Share of tab-switches that landed on already-prefetched chunks (`cacheHit = true`) over the last 14 days, broken down by tab. Low cache-hit % means `prefetchHubNavigationPages()` is not running early enough OR users navigate faster than prefetch completes.
panels.rationale: Sprint 0 carries an implicit hypothesis: tail-end ttiMs is dominated by cold-from-network switches. If cache-hit % is consistently >90% but P95 ttiMs is still bad, the bottleneck is mount/render, NOT chunk fetch — i.e. the prefetch optimization has already paid off, remaining win is in Sprint 1-3 code-splitting.

targets.Cache-hit baseline: ≥ 80% per tab — anything lower means prefetch race

query.query: SELECT properties.tab AS tab, countIf(properties.cacheHit = true) * 1.0 / count() AS cache_hit_ratio, count() AS sample_size FROM events WHERE event = 'hub_tab_switch_perf' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 14 DAY GROUP BY tab ORDER BY tab

panels.name: TTI histogram — distribution buckets
panels.description: Bucketed count of ttiMs values over the last 14 days. Buckets per runbook: 0–500, 500–1000, 1000–2000, 2000–5000, 5000–10000, 10000+. Visualizes the shape of the distribution — a bimodal cluster (one peak <500, one peak >5000) tells a different story than a single fat distribution centered at 3000.
panels.rationale: Averages and even percentiles can hide structure. A histogram with two peaks signals 'two distinct user experiences happening' (e.g. fresh-session cold cache vs subsequent-tab warm cache). Without this view, Sprint 1+ optimizations might improve one peak while leaving the other untouched and look like 50% wins instead of pinpoint fixes.

targets.Post-Sprint-3: Mass in 5000+ buckets should be ≤5% of total switches

query.query: SELECT properties.tab AS tab, multiIf(toFloat(properties.ttiMs) < 500, '0-500', toFloat(properties.ttiMs) < 1000, '500-1000', toFloat(properties.ttiMs) < 2000, '1000-2000', toFloat(properties.ttiMs) < 5000, '2000-5000', toFloat(properties.ttiMs) < 10000, '5000-10000', '10000+') AS bucket, count() AS switches FROM events WHERE event = 'hub_tab_switch_perf' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 14 DAY GROUP BY tab, bucket ORDER BY tab, bucket

panels.name: TTI trend — daily P50/P95 over 28 days
panels.description: Daily P50 + P95 ttiMs per tab over 28 days. Sprint-over-Sprint regression detector: when PR-1.1 merges, the curve should step down within 24h of the deploy. If it does not, the optimization landed but failed to bite (e.g. cache-hit dominated the prior baseline, or the lazy boundaries didn't change real-world perceived TTI).
panels.rationale: Static `Baseline` table in the runbook captures one snapshot. This trend tile captures the delta — and lets us correlate dips with deploy timestamps (cross-reference with Sergeant deploy log).

targets.Post-Sprint-1 deploy: step-down ≥30% within 48h of merge for affected tab
targets.Regression alarm: any +20% increase WoW → P2 ticket, investigate recent deploys

query.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) AS day, properties.tab AS tab, quantile(0.50)(toFloat(properties.ttiMs)) AS p50_ttiMs, quantile(0.95)(toFloat(properties.ttiMs)) AS p95_ttiMs, count() AS samples FROM events WHERE event = 'hub_tab_switch_perf' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 28 DAY GROUP BY day, tab ORDER BY day, tab

alerts.condition: P95 ttiMs for any tab increases ≥20% WoW (rolling 7-day windows)
alerts.channel: Telegram #ops (via n8n WF-16 ingest) + PostHog subscription
alerts.notes: Likely cause: recent deploy added eager-mount cost to a Settings/Reports section. Bisect via Sergeant deploy log.

alerts.condition: P95 longTaskCount exceeds Sprint-current target by 2× for 3 consecutive days
alerts.notes: Sprint 1 target = measured (no number yet); Sprint 2 = ≤5; Sprint 3 = ≤2. Trip-wire kicks in once Sprint-current target is set.

alerts.condition: cache_hit_ratio for any tab drops below 50% over 7 rolling days
alerts.notes: Likely cause: route-loader prefetch race (added in PR #3043). Re-investigate timing/scope.
